observed_problem: Lines 41-54 of /go's verify-task.py emitted an empty Python result for the same input that previously returned data; no exception, no stderr.
frequency: once so far; same input, same script, no code change between runs
proposed_change: Add a 'no silent empty output' warning to the existing verify-task pre-check: if the runner returned exit 0 with empty stdout and no stderr, surface 'VERIFY_EMPTY_RESULT' as a non-blocking diagnostic in the verify-artifact, so the next iteration can re-run with diagnostics on instead of treating it as success.

mechanism_trace.authority_or_verdict: verify-task's own exit code stays authoritative; the warning is a non-blocking diagnostic
mechanism_trace.freshness_check: assert_fresh(verify-result, run_id) — same per-run freshness invariant as the rest of /go state
mechanism_trace.failure_direction: advisory-only — the warning is surfaced to plain_english_report; it does NOT block .verified_
mechanism_trace.real_boundary_test: Spawn a synthetic run with the runner returning ('', '', 0) and confirm verify-result carries empty_result_warning=true and .verified_ is still touched; a unit test that mocks _run_verification is not sufficient

expected_benefit: Reduce silent-success detection lag from 'next session catches it' to 'same session surfaces it as a diagnostic'.
failure_mode_prevented: Silent empty-output success counted as a passing verification.
falsification_condition: If the warning fires on runs with non-empty output (FP) or misses real empty-output runs (FN) at a measurable rate, the detector needs calibration before any non-advisory use.

promotion_requirements.evidence_basis: Single observation; needs a reproduce-first test before any validator change.

items.description: Director confirms the warning is worth adding as advisory.
A regression test that reproduces the empty-output condition (real subprocess, not mock) must land before the warning ships.
